2574	\section{Pullback-monoidality: invertibility under a general scheme morphism}
2575	\label{sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality}
2576	
2577	The relative Picard consumer (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}) carries its line
2578	bundles on the \emph{local-triviality} predicate \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)
2579	(\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}): its carrier \(\mathtt{OnProduct}\) is the
2580	subobject \(\{\,L \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,(C \times_S T) \mid
2581	\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\,\}\) of locally-trivial \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules, and
2582	its functorial structure maps are \emph{pullback} maps of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules:
2583	the projection pullback \(\pi_T^*\) along \(C \times_S T \to T\) and the
2584	base-change pullback along \(C \times_S T' \to C \times_S T\). For these maps to
2585	descend to the carrier and to be \emph{additive} (an \(\mathtt{AddMonoidHom}\) of
2586	isomorphism classes) one needs the comparison isomorphism
2587	\[
2588	  f^*(L \otimes_X L')
2589	    \;\cong\;
2590	  f^*L \otimes_Y f^*L'
2591	\]
2592	for \emph{locally-trivial} \(L, L'\) and a \emph{general} morphism of schemes
2593	\(f : Y \to X\) --- the projection and the base-change morphism are neither open
2594	immersions nor flat. This restricted comparison isomorphism, on locally-trivial
2595	pairs, is the load-bearing result of the section
2596	(\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}); the invertibility corollary
2597	\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} is then a three-line consequence. The comparison
2598	is needed \emph{only} on locally-trivial pairs --- the carrier never presents a
2599	general \(\mathcal{O}\)-module --- so no comparison for arbitrary modules is
2600	required.
2601	
2602	This is a genuinely different fact from the open-immersion compatibility
2603	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, and not a corollary of it. That lemma handles
2604	restriction along an open immersion, and it does so by routing through the
2605	\emph{lax right} adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is
2606	strong monoidal there \emph{only} because an open immersion's structure-sheaf ring
2607	map is a local \emph{isomorphism} (\(\mathtt{appIso}\,f\)). A general scheme
2608	morphism has no such isomorphism, so the right-adjoint route is unavailable.
2609	
2610	\paragraph{The reduction to two facts about pullback.} The corollary
2611	\cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} is a three-line consequence of two facts about
2612	pullback, exactly as in the Stacks proof of \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}. The
2613	first is that pullback commutes with the tensor product on the \emph{locally-trivial}
2614	pairs the consumer presents: the canonical comparison
2615	\(f^*(L \otimes_X L') \cong f^*L \otimes_Y f^*L'\) is an isomorphism whenever
2616	\(L, L'\) are locally trivial, for every \(f\)
2617	(\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}). The second is that pullback preserves the
2618	structure sheaf, \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\)
2619	(\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, already in hand). Given a locally-trivial inverse
2620	\(N\) with \(L \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), pulling back yields
2621	\[
2622	  f^*L \otimes_Y f^*N
2623	    \;\cong\; f^*(L \otimes_X N)
2624	    \;\cong\; f^*\mathcal{O}_X
2625	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
2626	\]
2627	so \(f^*N\) is a tensor inverse of \(f^*L\). The locally-trivial comparison
2628	\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} is the load-bearing input.
2629	
2630	\paragraph{Why the apparent obstruction does not apply.} It is tempting to argue that
2631	the comparison can never be an isomorphism because an (op)lax monoidal functor need
2632	not preserve invertible objects --- global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal, yet
2633	\(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not invertible. But that
2634	counterexample concerns the \emph{lax} tensorator of \emph{pushforward}: \(\Gamma\)
2635	is a right adjoint (global sections), and its comparison
2636	\(\Gamma(\mathcal{F}) \otimes \Gamma(\mathcal{G}) \to \Gamma(\mathcal{F} \otimes
2637	\mathcal{G})\) genuinely fails to be an isomorphism. The comparison we need is the
2638	\emph{oplax} \(\delta\) of \emph{pullback}, a \emph{left} adjoint, running the other
2639	way, \(f^*(M \otimes N) \to f^*M \otimes f^*N\). Pullback \emph{provably preserves
2640	local triviality} (\cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}): if \(M\) is trivialised
2641	by \(\mathcal{O}_U\) on a cover \(\{U_i\}\), then \(f^*M\) is trivialised by
2642	\(\mathcal{O}_{f^{-1}U_i}\) on \(\{f^{-1}U_i\}\) --- using only
2643	\(f^*\mathcal{O} \cong \mathcal{O}\) and the compatibility of pullback with
2644	restriction along open immersions. Consequently, on locally-trivial objects, both
2645	sides of \(\delta\) are locally trivial, and \(\delta\) restricts to a comparison
2646	between the structure sheaf and itself on each chart, where it is forced to be an
2647	isomorphism. The bare oplax structure supplies \(\delta\) as a \emph{morphism} for
2648	free (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}); the entire remaining content is the
2649	chart-local check that, on a trivialising cover, \(\delta\) is an isomorphism.
2650	
2651	\paragraph{The construction route.} The isomorphism is obtained by \emph{upgrading
2652	the already-built oplax comparison map} \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} =
2653	\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) to an isomorphism
2654	through a chart-chase --- the same shape as the proven local-triviality results
2655	\cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback} and
2656	\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, and \emph{not} via any concrete
2657	inverse-image model or filtered-colimit construction. The argument decomposes into
2658	four steps, carried out in the sub-lemmas cited by
2659	\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}:
2660	\begin{itemize}
2661	  \item[(D1$'$)] \emph{Naturality of \(\delta\) in \((M,N)\).} The sheaf-level
2662	    comparison \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) is natural in both arguments, assembled from
2663	    the naturality of the abstract oplax \(\delta\) (Mathlib's
2664	    \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.}\delta\mathtt{\_natural}\)) and of the
2665	    sheafification reconciliations used to build it
2666	    (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}). This naturality is what lets a chart
2667	    replace \(M|_{U_i}, N|_{U_i}\) by the trivial bundle \(\mathcal{O}\) without
2668	    changing whether \(\delta\) is an isomorphism there.
2669	  \item[(D2$'$)] \emph{\(\delta\) on the unit pair \((\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})\) is an
2670	    isomorphism.} On the trivial bundle the comparison
2671	    \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})} : f^*(\mathcal{O} \otimes \mathcal{O}) \to
2672	    f^*\mathcal{O} \otimes f^*\mathcal{O}\) is reduced, by the \(\delta\)-wrapping
2673	    \(\mathtt{isIso\_sheafifyDelta\_unitPair\_of\_isIso\_sheafifyEta}\) (the presheaf
2674	    oplax left-unitality factored through sheafification, using
2675	    \(\mathtt{W\_of\_isIso\_sheafification}\) and the flatness-free right-whiskering
2676	    \(\mathtt{W\_whiskerRight\_of\_W}\)), to the single \emph{unit square}
2677	    \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F) \circ \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}
2678	    \circ \mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\) (\cref{lem:eta_bridge_unit_square}). That
2679	    square, transposed across the sheaf pullback--pushforward adjunction, bottoms out in
2680	    the structure-sheaf comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\)
2681	    (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}, an isomorphism for \emph{all} \(f\)); hence
2682	    \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})}\) is an isomorphism
2683	    (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}).
2684	  \item[(D3$'$)] \emph{Composition coherence of \(\delta\)} (the genuinely new
2685	    ingredient). For composable scheme morphisms \(h, f\) the comparison \(\delta\) for
2686	    the composite \(h \circ f\) factors through the comparisons for \(f\) and for \(h\),
2687	    conjugated by the pullback pseudofunctoriality isomorphism
2688	    \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}).
2689	    Specialising to \(h := j_i'\) at an open \(U_i\) of \(X\) with preimage
2690	    \(f^{-1}U_i\) --- with \(g_i : f^{-1}U_i \to U_i\) the restriction of \(f\) and
2691	    \(j_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\), \(j_i' : f^{-1}U_i \hookrightarrow Y\) the open
2692	    immersions, so \(f \circ j_i' = j_i \circ g_i\) --- this intertwines the

2699	    \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp\_}\delta\) and the conjugation

2700	    \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\) of the pseudofunctor isomorphisms.

2718	filtered-colimit/\(\otimes\) interchange, and no general comparison for arbitrary

2724	[The presheaf pushforward is lax monoidal]
2725	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}
2726	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal}
2727	  Let \(\varphi\) be a morphism of \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued ring presheaves,
2728	  presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules. Then the presheaf pushforward
2729	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\,\varphi\) is \emph{lax monoidal}: it
2730	  carries the comparison
2731	  \[
2732	    \mu :
2733	      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
2734	        \otimes (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
2735	      \;\longrightarrow\;
2736	      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,(A \otimes B),
2737	  \]
2738	  obtained as the composite of the strong-monoidal pushforward of presheaves over a
2739	  fixed ring presheaf (\(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\)) with the lax
2740	  monoidal restriction of scalars \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) along the
2741	  ring-presheaf map. This is a statement at the \emph{presheaf} level of
2742	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules; it does not require a monoidal structure on
2743	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\).
2744	\end{lemma}
2745	
2746	\begin{proof}
2747	
2748	
2749	
2750	  Restriction of scalars along a homomorphism of ring presheaves is lax monoidal --- the
2751	  comparison sends \((a \otimes b)\) to itself under the identification of the
2752	  restricted tensor product with the source tensor product --- and the pushforward of
2753	  presheaves of modules over a \emph{fixed} ring presheaf is strong monoidal. The
2754	  presheaf pushforward along \(\varphi\) factors as the composite of these two functors,
2755	  so its lax monoidal structure is the composite of the strong-monoidal structure on the
2756	  first factor with the lax structure on the second; the comparison \(\mu\) is the
2757	  whiskered composite of the two comparisons.
2758	\end{proof}

2807	\begin{lemma}
2808	[Pullback commutes with $\otimes$ for a general scheme morphism]
2809	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}
2810	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIso}
2811	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
2812	        lem:pullback_lan_decomposition, lem:pullback0_tensor_iso,
2813	        lem:pullback_tensor_map}
2814	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
2815	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
2816	  Writing \(f^* = \mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f\) for the inverse-image functor
2817	  of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules, the canonical comparison
2818	  \[
2819	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2820	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2821	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N)
2822	  \]
2823	  is an isomorphism, natural in \(M\) and \(N\). No local-freeness, flatness, or
2824	  open-immersion hypothesis is required: it holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
2825	  and all morphisms \(f\). This is the general strong-monoidality of pullback; its
2826	  underlying morphism is the comparison map \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}, here
2827	  upgraded to an isomorphism.
2828	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
2829	  % \ref{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} (pullback of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a
2830	  % morphism of ringed spaces commutes with tensor product, functorially)
2831	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2392-2400).
2832	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2833	  % "\begin{lemma}
2834	  % \label{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}
2835	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be
2836	  % a morphism of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$
2837	  % be $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules. Then
2838	  % $f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_Y} \mathcal{G})
2839	  % = f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} f^*\mathcal{G}$
2840	  % functorially in $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$.
2841	  % \end{lemma}"
2842	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
2843	  \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}: for a morphism of ringed spaces
2844	  \(f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)\) and \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-modules
2845	  \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\), one has \(f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes \mathcal{G}) =
2846	  f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes f^*\mathcal{G}\) functorially.}
2847	\end{lemma}
2848	
2849	\begin{proof}
2850	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
2851	        lem:pullback_lan_decomposition, lem:pullback0_tensor_iso,
2852	        lem:pullback_tensor_map}
2853	  Mathematically, pullback is extension of scalars and extension of scalars is strong
2854	  monoidal, so the comparison is an isomorphism for every \(f\). The only work is to
2855	  carry this through the formalisation's \emph{abstract} pullback
2856	  \(f^* = (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\), which exposes no sectionwise
2857	  value. We do not evaluate \(f^*\) sectionwise; we equip a \emph{concrete} model of the
2858	  pullback with a strong-monoidal structure and transport it to \(f^*\) along the
2859	  uniqueness of left adjoints. This mirrors Mathlib's construction of the
2860	  extension-of-scalars monoidal structure, where the
2861	  \emph{left} adjoint's strong-monoidal structure is built explicitly (via the
2862	  base-change distributivity isomorphism) and the right adjoint's lax structure is
2863	  derived from the adjunction. The argument has four steps.
2864	
2865	  \emph{Step 1 (D1: decomposition).} The presheaf pushforward factors as a fixed-ring
2866	  pushforward followed by restriction of scalars,
2867	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi = \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \circ
2868	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\). Taking left adjoints reverses the composite, so the
2869	  presheaf pullback factors as
2870	  \[
2871	    \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi \;\cong\; \mathtt{extendScalars}\,\varphi \circ
2872	    \mathtt{pullback}_0, \qquad
2873	    \mathtt{pullback}_0 = (\mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R).\mathtt{leftAdjoint},
2874	  \]
2875	  with \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) the topological inverse image
2876	  (\cref{lem:pullback_lan_decomposition}).
2877	
2878	  \emph{Step 2 (D2: the scalar half is strong, for free).} Extension of scalars
2879	  \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\varphi\) is strong monoidal: its tensorator is the
2880	  base-change distributivity isomorphism
2881	  \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange}\),
2882	  \[
2883	    (M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S
2884	      \;\cong\;
2885	    (M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S (N \otimes_R S),
2886	  \]
2887	  an isomorphism for \emph{every} ring map \(R \to S\), with no flatness. Composing a
2888	  strong-monoidal functor with \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\), the comparison \(\delta\) for
2889	  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is therefore an isomorphism \emph{if and only if} the
2890	  comparison \(\delta_0\) for \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is. This isolates the topological
2891	  half as the sole genuine obligation.
2892	
2893	  \emph{Step 3 (D3: the topological half --- the genuine build).} The functor
2894	  \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is the left Kan extension
2895	  \(\mathrm{Lan}\,(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\) on underlying
2896	  presheaves (its right adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\) is restriction along
2897	  \(F^{\mathrm{op}}\)). Pointwise, with \(F = (\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\),
2898	  \[
2899	    \mathtt{pullback}_0(M \otimes N)(V)
2900	      = \operatorname*{colim}_{(F \downarrow V)} \bigl(M(U) \otimes N(U)\bigr),
2901	    \quad
2902	    (\mathtt{pullback}_0 M \otimes \mathtt{pullback}_0 N)(V)
2903	      = \Bigl(\operatorname*{colim} M(U)\Bigr) \otimes \Bigl(\operatorname*{colim} N(U)\Bigr).
2904	  \]
2905	  The comma category \((F \downarrow V) = \{U : f^{-1}V \subseteq U\}\) is up-directed
2906	  --- \(U_1 \cup U_2\) is an upper bound of any \(U_1, U_2\) --- and the tensor product
2907	  commutes with filtered colimits (the diagonal \((F\downarrow V) \to (F\downarrow V)
2908	  \times (F \downarrow V)\) is final). Hence the comparison \(\delta_0\) is an
2909	  isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback0_tensor_iso}). This is the genuine content of the lemma.
2910	
2911	  \emph{Step 4 (D4: sheafify and transport).} Steps 1--3 furnish a strong-monoidal
2912	  structure on the concrete presheaf-level inverse image. Sheafify the resulting presheaf
2913	  isomorphism to the sheaf level --- the unit and tensor factors are reconciled with
2914	  their sheafified forms by the sheafification-monoidality brick
2915	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\), the same \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)
2916	  shape used in \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. By definition
2917	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f = (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\)
2918	  is \emph{a} left adjoint of the pushforward; the uniqueness of left adjoints
2919	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) supplies a canonical natural isomorphism from it
2920	  to the concrete model, along which the strong-monoidal structure travels. Its
2921	  tensorator becomes the asserted isomorphism
2922	  \[
2923	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2924	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2925	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
2926	  \]
2927	  natural in \(M\) and \(N\), refining the comparison map
2928	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) of
2929	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}; the abstract pullback is never evaluated sectionwise.
2930	
2931	  No shortcut from the bare oplax map is available: the presheaf pullback is oplax
2932	  monoidal (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) and so supplies \(\delta\) as a
2933	  morphism for free, but an (op)lax monoidal functor does not preserve invertible objects
2934	  --- global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal yet \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1,
2935	  \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not invertible --- so the isomorphism must be proved, which is
2936	  exactly the work of Steps~1--4.
2937	\end{proof}

2951	  functor \(F\) of ring presheaves. The presheaf pushforward factors as the fixed-ring
2952	  pushforward followed by restriction of scalars,
2953	  \[

2955	      \;=\;

2959	  \[

2967	  extension along \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\) --- and

2978	  composite of the left adjoints in the reverse order; the left adjoint of

2980	  left adjoint of \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R\) is by definition

2983	  isomorphism. That \(\mathtt{pullback}_0\) is the left Kan extension along
2984	  \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base})^{\mathrm{op}}\) follows because its right adjoint

3181	
3182	
3183	  Both sides are morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules; it suffices to compare them on

3193	\begin{lemma}
3194	
3195	\leanok
3196	[Composition coherence of the pullback unit comparison]
3197	  \label{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}

3199	  \uses{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}

3201	  \(\mathrm{pbu}\) for the pullback (left-adjoint) unit comparison
3202	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\), the canonical map
3203	  \(f^*\mathcal{O} \to \mathcal{O}\). Then \(\mathrm{pbu}\) satisfies the composition
3204	  coherence
3205	  \[
3206	    \mathrm{pbu}(h \mathbin{;} f)
3207	      \;=\;
3208	    (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv} \;\mathbin{;}\;
3209	      (\mathtt{pullback}\,h).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
3210	      \mathrm{pbu}\,h,

3212	  where \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f : (h \mathbin{;} f)^* \cong h^* \circ f^*\) is the
3213	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism of the pullback. This is the genuinely-new ingredient

3215	  no sectionwise value, so this identity is \emph{not} a sectionwise statement and cannot

3219	\begin{proof}
3220	  \uses{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}
3221	  The pullback \(f^*\) is the left adjoint of the pushforward \(f_*\), and likewise for
3222	  \(h\) and \(h \mathbin{;} f\); the comparison \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) is the adjunction
3223	  \emph{mate} of the pushforward comparison \(\mathrm{upu}\,f\)
3224	  (\cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}) across the pullback--pushforward adjunction,
3225	  and similarly for \(h\) and \(h \mathbin{;} f\). Since the mate correspondence is a
3226	  bijection, the asserted identity follows once both sides are transposed under the
3227	  adjunction \((h \mathbin{;} f)^* \dashv (h \mathbin{;} f)_*\).
3228	
3229	  Under this transpose the left-hand side \(\mathrm{pbu}(h \mathbin{;} f)\) becomes the
3230	  pushforward comparison \(\mathrm{upu}(h \mathbin{;} f)\). The right-hand side, a
3231	  composite involving \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv}\) and the mates of
3232	  \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) and \(\mathrm{pbu}\,h\), transposes --- using the conjugation of the
3233	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphisms (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f\) on the left adjoints
3234	  corresponds to \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f\) on the right adjoints) and the unit of

3236	  right-hand side of the pushforward coherence
3237	  \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}, namely
3238	  \(\mathrm{upu}\,f \mathbin{;} f_*(\mathrm{upu}\,h) \mathbin{;}
3239	  (\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{hom}\). Thus the two transposed sides coincide
3240	  by \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}, and the original identity follows by the

3244	\begin{lemma}
3245	
3246	\leanok
3247	[Pullback preserves the structure sheaf]
3248	  \label{lem:pullback_unit_iso}
3249	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso}
3250	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
3251	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes. The pullback of the structure sheaf
3252	  is the structure sheaf: there is a canonical isomorphism
3253	  \[
3254	    f^*\bigl(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\bigr)
3255	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
3256	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,Y.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf},
3257	    \qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
3258	    f^* \mathcal{O}_X \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
3259	  \]
3260	  where \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf} = \mathcal{O}_X\) is
3261	  the monoidal unit of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\).
3262	\end{lemma}
3263	
3264	\begin{proof}
3265	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
3266	  This is a one-step consequence of a Mathlib instance, with no chart-chase. The
3267	  canonical comparison map is the genuine Mathlib morphism
3268	  \[
3269	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,f :
3270	      f^*\mathcal{O}_X \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
3271	  \]
3272	  the unit comparison \(\eta\) of \(f^*\); abbreviate it \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\). Mathlib
3273	  proves this map is an isomorphism whenever the comparison functor
3274	  \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}\) on opens is \(\mathtt{Final}\): the instance
3275	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal}\).
3276	
3277	  The point is that the finality hypothesis holds \emph{unconditionally}, for every
3278	  morphism of schemes \(f\). The preimage functor \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}
3279	  : \mathtt{Opens}\,X \to \mathtt{Opens}\,Y\) is a frame homomorphism: it preserves
3280	  arbitrary unions and finite intersections, hence preserves all finite limits. A
3281	  functor between small categories that preserves finite limits is representably flat,
3282	  so \(\mathtt{final\_of\_representablyFlat}\) gives \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}).\mathtt{Final}\)
3283	  with no hypothesis on \(f\) whatsoever. (There is no need to pass to affine charts:
3284	  finality is not a special feature of open immersions but a general property of
3285	  preimage-on-opens.)
3286	
3287	  Consequently the instance \(\mathtt{instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal}\) fires
3288	  directly, and \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) is an isomorphism for every \(f\). The asserted
3289	  isomorphism \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\) is the bundled iso
3290	  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) of this comparison.

3296	[Naturality of the sheaf-level pullback--tensor comparison $\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}$ (D1$'$)]
3297	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}

3299	  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}

3302	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is \emph{natural} in both module arguments: for

3305	  \[
3306	    \begin{array}{ccc}
3307	      f^*(M \otimes_X N)
3308	        & \xrightarrow{\;\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\;}
3309	        & f^*M \otimes_Y f^*N \\[2pt]
3310	      \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f^*(a \otimes b)}
3311	        & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f^*a \,\otimes\, f^*b} \\[4pt]
3312	      f^*(M' \otimes_X N')
3313	        & \xrightarrow{\;\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\;}

3320	\begin{proof}
3321	  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal,
3322	        lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural, lem:pullback_val_iso_natural}
3323	  The map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) is built (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) by
3324	  transporting the abstract oplax comparison \(\delta\) of the presheaf pullback
3325	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) through the sheafification
3326	  reconciliations. The asserted square is the pasting of four naturality squares, one for
3327	  each ingredient of this composite; we record them in order, the only delicate one being the
3328	  naturality of the oplax comparison itself.
3329	
3330	  \emph{Square 2: naturality of the oplax comparison \(\delta\).} The comparison \(\delta\) is
3331	  the oplax-monoidal comparison of the presheaf-level pullback functor
3332	  \(F = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi'\), whose defining ring map
3333	  \(\varphi' = (\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\) has, as the source of its domain,
3334	  the ring presheaf of \(X\). For the monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) to
3335	  be available on this domain the ring presheaf must be presented in its \emph{canonical}
3336	  form as the composite
3337	  \(\mathcal{O}_X \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\) --- the
3338	  presentation on which that monoidal structure is defined. When the comparison map's
3339	  construction is unfolded, however, the domain ring re-presents in a form definitionally
3340	  equal, but not explicitly identical, to the canonical one, and on that form the
3341	  monoidal structure is not directly available. The naturality of \(\delta\),
3342	  \[
3343	    \delta \circ F(a \otimes b) \;=\; (Fa \otimes Fb) \circ \delta,
3344	  \]
3345	  which holds for any oplax monoidal functor, is therefore applied by re-establishing the
3346	  canonical domain-ring spelling at the functor argument itself: one ascribes the defining
3347	  ring map \(\varphi'\) explicitly to the canonical composite-functor source, so that the
3348	  domain monoidal structure resolves, and the naturality identity then matches the goal
3349	  definitionally. This is a structural device --- it changes only the presentation of
3350	  \(F\)'s defining ring map. No restatement of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) or of its helper
3351	  isomorphisms is needed, and the unit-case lemma \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap\_unit\_isIso}\)
3352	  (D2\(^\prime\)) is unaffected. After this step the comparison \(\delta\) commutes past
3353	  \(F(a \otimes b)\), producing the factor \(Fa \otimes Fb\).
3354	
3355	  \emph{Squares 1, 3, 4 and bifunctoriality.} The remaining ingredients of
3356	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) are honest natural transformations and contribute already-closed
3357	  naturality squares: the sheafification, the tensor/unit reconciliation
3358	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) (Square 3, \cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural}),
3359	  and the \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reconciliation (Square 4,
3360	  \cref{lem:pullback_val_iso_natural}). Finally, the tensor of the two component morphisms
3361	  factors as \(f^*a \otimes f^*b\) by the bifunctoriality of \(\otimes_Y\) --- the interchange
3362	  law \((f_1 \otimes f_2) \circ (g_1 \otimes g_2) = (f_1 \circ g_1) \otimes (f_2 \circ g_2)\),
3363	  here \(\mathtt{tensorHom\_comp\_tensorHom}\). Pasting the four squares together with this
3364	  bifunctoriality identity yields the asserted commuting square.
3365	\end{proof}

3367	\begin{lemma}
3368	
3369	\leanok
3370	  [Section-level naturality of the tensor/unit reconciliation
3371	   \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\)]
3372	  \label{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural}

3374	  The tensor/unit reconciliation isomorphism \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) entering the
3375	  fourth naturality square of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} is natural in both
3376	  module arguments. Equivalently: after descending to sections over each open \(U\) and
3377	  passing to the underlying module components, the naturality square is the
3378	  bilinear, sectionwise naturality of the sheafification unit \(\eta\), verified one pure
3379	  tensor \(p \otimes q\) at a time, using bilinearity.

3381	
3382	\begin{proof}
3383	  \uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map}
3384	  Write \(\Phi\) for the natural transformation whose naturality square (in the two module
3385	  arguments \(M, N\), along morphisms \(a : M \to M'\) and \(b : N \to N'\)) is to be shown
3386	  to commute; \(\Phi\) is assembled from the reconciliation isomorphism
3387	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) (the intermediate construction inside
3388	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}), so its components are morphisms of presheaves of modules.
3389	  The square is closed at the categorical (tensor-of-morphisms) level, without any descent to
3390	  sections or to elements.
3391	
3392	  \emph{Step 1: a single tensor-of-morphisms presentation.} The \(\mathrm{hom}\) leg of
3393	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) is rewritten as a \emph{single} tensor of morphisms of the
3394	  form \(a.\mathrm{map}(\eta_P \otimes \eta_Q)\), where
3395	  \(\eta = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}).\mathrm{unit}\) is the unit
3396	  \(\mathbf{1} \Rightarrow (\text{sheafify}) \circ (\text{inclusion})\) of the sheafification
3397	  adjunction and \(a\) is the sheafification functor. The essential point is that all factors
3398	  are kept inside \emph{one} monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) --- the
3399	  canonical presentation as a composite with the forgetful functor \(\mathtt{forget}_2\) ---
3400	  so that the bifunctoriality law of \(\otimes\) can be applied consistently within one
3401	  monoidal structure.

3404	  images, under the sheafification functor, of a tensor of morphisms. The two functor-image
3405	  factors are merged into a single image of a composite by functoriality,
3406	  \(F(g) \circ F(h) = F(g \circ h)\).
3407	
3408	  \emph{Step 3: bifunctoriality and unit naturality.} The resulting equality of two tensors of
3409	  morphisms is closed by the bifunctoriality (interchange) law
3410	  \[
3411	    (f_1 \otimes f_2) \circ (g_1 \otimes g_2) \;=\; (f_1 \circ g_1) \otimes (f_2 \circ g_2),
3412	  \]

3414	  single-component naturality squares of the sheafification unit \(\eta\), one in each module

3422	  single-component squares through the interchange law equates the two tensors of morphisms,

3466	  expresses \(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,\mathbf{1}\,\mathbf{1}\) as a composite of

3470	  isomorphisms, so after sheafification by \(a_Y\) the genuine remaining content is

3473	  \]
3474	  the sheafified presheaf unit comparison (the \(\delta\)-wrapping reduction). By \cref{lem:isiso_sheafifyeta_of_unitsquare} it suffices, in turn, to prove the
3475	  \emph{unit square}

3488	  delivers that \(\delta_{(\mathcal{O},\mathcal{O})}\) is an isomorphism.
